data.title: Synovus, UMB Financial get new Buy ratings at Citi in a look at regional banks
data.description:
Citi initiated coverage of four regional banks that have attracted more investor interest after recent M&A transactions with Buy ratings on Synovus Financial (NYSE:SNV) and UMB Financial (NASDAQ:UMBF).

Analyst Benjamin Gerlinger assigned Neutral ratings to Banc of California (NYSE:BANC) and SouthState (NYSE:SSB).

While Synovus (SNV) is likely to track the national trend of "somewhat sluggish" loan growth in Q3, its lending hires branch footprint boasting higher than the national average GDP outlook should generate multiyear loan growth that outpaces its peers.

Gerlinger sees "substantial opportunities" after the company's acquisition of Heartland Financial (HTLF) to generate market-leading return on tangible common equity and to capture market share.

In addition, "we believe the combination of these two balance sheets is being overly discounted by consensus expectations," the analyst wrote in a note to clients.

Meanwhile, Banc of California's (BANC) liability-sensitive balance sheet modest valuation premium and compressing expense outlook already appears to be priced into its valuation.

SouthState's (SSB) premium valuation leaves little room for further multiple expansion near term. Citi prefers to wait for a more attractive entry point.

However, the SA Quant rating prefers SouthState (SSB) over Synovus (SNV) and UMB Financial (UMBF). The SA Quant system has no rating on Banc of California (BANC).

In Thursday morning trading, Synovus (SNV) stock rose 0.7%, UMB Financial (UMBF) was little changed, Banc of California (BANC) dropped 1.3%, and SouthState (SSB) fell 0.7%.

data.title: Analysts maintain rating on Netflix as Q3 earnings near, anticipate U.S. price hikes
data.description:
Wall Street analysts mostly stayed bullish on streaming giant Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) and weighed in with their commentary ahead of its third quarter earnings.

Oppenheimer said the company remains "the dominant streaming platform" and maintains the largest market share of U.S. TV viewership. The ratings firm believes NFLX's initiatives, such as password sharing rules, advertising, and optimizing subscriber plan choices, will drive subscriber growth and average revenue per membership, leading to higher revenue.

Oppenheimer noted that the company announced an increase to the Premium tier in the US, UK, and France last October and now anticipates a hike in Premium pricing for other regions, and more importantly, an 8%–15% increase to the Standard plan.

To reflect pricing increase, they forecast ad revenue of $900M and 33M ad-tier subs for 2025 and $1.8B revenue and 41M ad-tier subs in 2026. For Q3, the expect EPS of $5.10 and revenue of $9.8B, and for the full-year, EPS of $19.32 and revenue of $38.9B.

"We believe NFLX’s dominance will continue, given its clear advantage in producing high-engagement content and monetizing that content more effectively than peers," Oppenheimer said in its October 9 research note.

Morgan Stanley said it is bullish on NFLX shares as it sees a long runway for revenue growth, above consensus expectations for operating leverage and EPS, and a deepening competitive moat, among things.

"The key to Netflix's success stems from generating more value from each dollar invested in content. Content amortization is its largest expense item (over 50% of its opex base in '24), and leveraging this investment is key to long-term earnings growth. Throughout the roller coaster that has been 2019 to 2024, its revenues have grown at a +14% CAGR vs. cash content spend of just +3%," Morgan Stanley analysts said in their October 9 note.

Citi Research, on the other hand, stayed "neutral" on NFLX and said they see a possibility for the company to raise its prices in the U.S. by 12%, given its low cost per viewed hour.

However, they also pointed out that NFLX may require revenue grow of ~15% if it has to generate $25 of EPS in 2025. They argued that even if Netflix adds 20M subs and raises U.S. prices by 12%, non-U.S. ARPUs would need to rise 6% to achieve ~15% revenue growth in 2025, which they view as unlikely.

"We expect Netflix’s stock to trade higher on a U.S. price hike announcement, but we would expect shares to eventually trade lower as investor’s hopes for $25 in 2025 EPS are dashed," Citi said.

Oppenheimer has given NFLX an "outperform" rating and a price target of $775, hiked from $725, implying an upside of 6.5%. Morgan Stanley rated it "overweight" and raised PT to $820, from $780, a 13% upside.

Citi had its PT unchanged at $675, implying a 7% downside. Stock is up 49% so far this year while the S&P 500 rose 21%. The company is scheduled to report Q3 earnings on October 17.

More on Netflix

data.title: Air India is said to be source of 85-plane order at Airbus
data.description:
Airbus (OTCPK:EADSY) (OTCPK:EADSF) in September received an order for 85 more planes from Air India, Bloomberg News reported Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The order included 75 A320neos single-aisle planes and 10 A350 wide-body jets.

Airbus (OTCPK:EADSY) (OTCPK:EADSF) this week said it had delivered 50 aircraft in September and booked 235 orders from customers, including 85 from an undisclosed buyer.

Air India, which is owned by Tata Group, is currently absorbing Vistara, a joint venture carrier the Tata Group started with Singapore Airlines. The carrier has ordered new jets amid an effort to expand its share of India’s booming domestic and strong outbound markets.

data.title: Energy jolt: Celsius Holdings and Monster Beverage gain after trade show buzz
data.description:
Celsius Holdings (NASDAQ:CELH) broke higher on Thursday after Stifel pointed to some positive developments from the National Association of Convenience Stores trade show.

Analyst Mark Astrachan said conversations with brand owners and retailers at the convention were more upbeat in tone on the potential for convenience store category sales to improve in 2025. Astrachan reminded investors that 62% of energy drink sales are from the convenience store channel. As for Q4, Astrachan said that while energy drink category trends will be impacted by weather in the southeast U.S., Stifel expects to see generally improving sales trends reflecting more favorable comparisons, innovation, and pricing. Astrachan and his team expect Celsius Holdings (CELH) to match rival Monster Beverage (NASDAQ:MNST) and raise prices in the mid-single digits.

Shares of Celsius Holdings (CELH) were up 11.6% in late morning trading on Thursday and are swapping hands at their highest level in three weeks. The rally coincides with short interest on CELH being at an elevated position of 10.0% of the total float. Monster Beverage (MNST) edged 0.7% higher on Thursday as it continues to recover from its 52-week low struck in August.

data.title: CareDx stock climbs 7% amid reports Natera not pursuing litigation
data.description:
Shares of CareDx (NASDAQ:CDNA) climbed 7% Thursday amid news Natera (NTRA) was no longer pursuing infringement claims for two patents against CareDx’s current version of AlloSure Kidney.

“With no claims of ongoing infringement, Natera (NTRA) cannot obtain an injunction on the current version of AlloSure Kidney,” Craig-Hallum said in a note Thursday, adding the news should be a “positive catalyst for CDNA shares as the Natera motion was seen by some as an overhang on the stock.”

Shares of CareDx (CDNA) closed 12% lower on Wednesday. Craig-Hallum said it believed the drop was likely due to a large seller triggering quant selling.

Also on Wednesday, CareDx (CDNA) said it had appointed Jing Huang as its chief data and artificial intelligence officer, a newly created position. Huang will lead data science initiatives that are a part of the company’s long-term strategic growth plan, CareDx (CDNA) said in a statement.

On Tuesday, CareDx (CDNA) announced that the U.S. Department of Justice had closed an investigation into the company with no finding of wrongdoing.

CareDx plans to present it long-term growth strategy during an Investor Day on October 15.

data.title: S&P 500 tags its 44th record high in 2024 with 56 trading days remaining
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SP500) set its 44th record closing high of the year on Wednesday, marking a significant milestone in 2024's stock market performance. With 56 trading days remaining in the year, this places 2024 in the 11th position for the highest number of record closes in a single year since 1954.

This achievement suggests that 2024 is on track to be one of the stronger years for the S&P 500, though it's still trailing behind some standout years like 1995, which holds the record with 77 closing highs. The remaining 56 trading days leave room for additional records, which could potentially boost 2024's rank in the all-time standings.

See the below chart that Bespoke Investment Group provided:

Furthermore, the S&P 500 trades at 5,781 and is higher on the year by 21.8% and over the past month alone the index has advanced as much as 5.3%.

For market participants aiming to further monitor the pulse of the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500), they can track both exchange-traded funds and mutual funds as a proxy for exposure towards the market index.

Some funds worth analyzing are as follows: (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (SSO), (RSP), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (FXAIX), (VFIAX), and (VFFSX).

data.title: Microsoft in focus as Morgan Stanley, Goldman Sachs weigh in ahead of earnings
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) was in focus on Thursday as Morgan Stanley and Goldman Sachs weighed in on the tech giant ahead of its quarterly earnings, slated to be released in short order.

Morgan Stanley analyst Keith Weiss, who has an Overweight rating and $506 price target on the tech giant, said a recent CIO survey showed "stable" software growth expectations for 2024 and a "moderate" acceleration in growth expected for 2025 software budgets, with artificial intelligence and machine learning at the top of CIO's minds.

"Importantly, hyperscalers remain the favored vendor group for CIOs in assisting to apply AI, LLM's, and other innovative technologies," Weiss wrote in a note.

"And critically, Microsoft is in the leadership position, driven in part by: strong alignment to key secular themes and CIO priorities; vast scope of products to monetize Generative AI across its broad portfolio and installed base; ability to leverage consumption pricing (via its Azure Cloud business) enabling investors to see pick-up in demand translate to revenue earlier – this translates to Microsoft expanding its leadership position as the #1 share gainer of IT wallet on a 1- year and 3-year view as workloads shift to the cloud and its leadership position allows it to capture the largest incremental share of GenAI spend by a wide margin."

Goldman Sachs analyst Kash Rangan noted that the coming quarterly results — especially free cash flow — could be impacted by a step-up in AI capex spending.

"We note in prior periods of accelerated CapEx and GM pressure, the stock underperformed in the [short-term], while ultimately outperforming with Azure re-acceleration; we could now be at a similar juncture," Rangan wrote.

"However, to better reflect our expectations for the CapEx build out to mirror that of Azure, we raise our CapEx estimates (with the initial step-up expanded in each subsequent year). Though we are modeling $2-3bn in OpenAI losses for FY25/FY26, we see room for tailwinds to EPS from operating efficiencies as Microsoft’s strategic investments scale."

Rangan reiterated his Buy rating but lowered his price target to $500 from $515.

A consensus of analysts expect Microsoft to earn $3.10 per share on $64.52B for the coming quarter.

data.title: ADMA Biologics drops as auditor resigns
data.description:
Shares of ADMA Biologics (NASDAQ:ADMA) fell sharply in the morning on Thursday after the plasma-based treatment maker announced that CohnReznick LLP had decided to resign as the company’s independent public auditor.

ADMA, headquartered in Ramsey, New Jersey, said it has started a process to find a new accounting firm to audit the company’s financials and internal controls for the year ending December 31, 2024, on time, adhering to filing deadlines.

“CohnReznick is committed to collaborating with and supporting the Company and its newly engaged independent registered public accounting firm, once appointed, to expedite the new audit firm’s smooth transition,” ADMA said in a regulatory filing on Wednesday after the close.

The company added in a regulatory filing on Thursday that the auditor transition will not impact its previously disclosed financial guidance for 2024-2025.

The resignation will become effective with the filing of the company’s 10-Q for the fiscal quarter ending September 30, 2024.

data.title: Goldman sees a ‘relief rally’ potential for these option calls ahead of earnings
data.description:
Goldman Sachs analysts listed 25 companies where they see the most “out of consensus” moves for the upcoming quarterly earnings.

Options prices have risen due to the geopolitical environment, and ahead of seasonally high earnings volatility, the U.S. presidential election, and the November FOMC meeting, wrote John Marshall, head of derivatives research at Goldman, in a note.

He said that Goldman analysts see potential for upward earnings revisions in 18 names Gap (GAP), Amgen (AMGN), and Nvidia (NVDA), and potential for downward earnings revisions for seven names including Cheniere Energy (CQP), and Kraft Heinz (KHC).

In addition, option implied earnings-day-moves for S&P 500 (SP500) stocks average at about 6% this quarter, above their 18-year mean, “demonstrating elevated nervousness ahead of results,” said Marshall. Also, earnings-day-moves reached a 14-year high last quarter.

The following are the 25 most out-of-consensus opportunities within U.S. stocks:

  • Gap Inc. (GAP) – Implied stock move: 7.6%; Option call: December term, strike 21.00, cost 9.8%

data.title: S&P 500 real estate picks by Seeking Alpha's Quant ahead of Q3 earnings season
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating system gives the S&P 500 real estate stocks an average health score of 3.19 on a scale of 5 ahead of the Q3 earnings season.

Quant awards grades based on quantitative measures, like valuation, earnings growth and recent stock performance. The highest possible score for any individual company is a 5.

Real estate stocks accounted for the second-biggest growth among the S&P 500 sectors in Q3. XLRE gained 17.43% in Q3, outpacing the broader S&P 500 Index's 5.25% rise.

The sector's performance was marked by the Federal Open Market Committee trimming its benchmark interest rate by 50 basis points to 4.75%-5.0% in September. The real estate commission rules that went into effect in August had an impact.

Quant Ratings

Among the individual sector companies, real estate services provider CBRE Group (CBRE) (Strong Buy with a score of 4.67), specialized REIT VICI Properties (VICI) (Strong Buy with a score of 4.64) and retail REIT Regency Centers (REG) (Strong Buy with a score of 4.61) are the top picks.

Telecom tower REITs SBA Communications (SBAC) (Strong Sell with a score of 1.34) and Crown Castle (CCI) (Sell with a score of 2.00) and timber REIT Weyerhaeuser (WY) (Strong Sell with a score of 1.47) rank the least.

With the highest index weight of 10.00%, industrial REIT Prologis (PLD) gets a Hold rating and a score of 2.99. Telecom tower REIT American Tower (AMT), holding the second-highest index weight of 9.48%, scores 3.32 with a Hold rating.

The Real Estate Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:XLRE) holds a Quant score of 2.89 with a Hold rating, the eighth-highest place among the 11 sector-based ETFs.

Quant grades the fund as B for Momentum, A for Expenses, C- for Dividends, D+ for Risk and A+ for Liquidity.

Out of the 31 S&P 500 real estate stocks, four are rated as Strong Buys, four as Buys, 20 as Neutrals, one as Sell and 2 as Strong Sells.

Analyst Say

The real estate sector is expected to report an earnings growth of 5% in Q3, while the estimated growth rate for the S&P 500 index is 4.2%, FactSet data said.

The quarterly sector revenue is estimated to grow by 4.7%, the same level as SP500, the data showed.

For Q3, analysts have cut their bottom-up EPS estimates for the sector by 2.1%.

RBC Capital Markets maintains an Underweight rating on REITs.
